steps.title: Connect memory and regret
steps.content: The speaker wishes she could remember the first meeting but says it slipped away unrecorded. She explains that she was unable to foresee that the relationship would later blossom, which creates a regretful tone.

steps.title: Avoid overstated readings
steps.content: A turns the poem into celebration, though the emphasis is regret over lost memory. C invents an anniversary. D says the speaker neglected or may lose the relationship, but the poem instead suggests it later became important. The best statement links forgotten beginnings with not anticipating future importance. The answer is B.
